summary: Iteration 7 — geolocation + 30-min photo edit window. BACKEND: 17/17 NEW iter7 pytest passing (test_geolocation_iter7.py). Covered: PATCH pinpad WITHOUT lat/lng allowed when more pin pads remain → 200; WITHOUT lat/lng on the LAST pinpad → 400 'Ubicación requerida'; WITH lat/lng on the last pinpad → 200, estado=finalizada, closed_lat/lng/address/accuracy_m + finalized_at persisted on the orden root AND on the pin pad itself. PATCH /foto replace within window → 200 (edited_at set, photo and notas updated). PATCH /foto with uploaded_at backdated 31 min in DB → 403 'ventana expiró'. DELETE /foto: 400 when orden ya finalizada; 400 when pin pad not completed; 200 happy-path resets completed=False AND clears evidencia_base64, notas, completed_at, uploaded_at, lat, lng, address; 403 when window expired. PATCH /finalizar without lat/lng → 422 Pydantic; with lat/lng → 200 + all closed_* fields persisted. AUTH matrix: 401 sin token, 403 admin token, 403 foreign técnico — for both PATCH pinpad and DELETE /foto and PATCH /foto. REGRESSION: Fixed 1 stale test in test_mvg_backend.py (test_tecnico_only_sees_own_ordenes) that was sending finalizar without lat/lng and expecting 400 — updated to send lat/lng (and added an extra 422-when-missing-geo assertion). Remaining 6 failures in test_mvg_iter2 are PRE-EXISTING (mitiendapro returns 500 for fake +56912345678 phones, documented since iter6). Total pytest: 94/100 passing — the 6 stale failures are NOT regressions from iter7. FRONTEND (mobile 390x844, técnico TEST_geo_d7039e@mvg.cl): orden detail renders 2 pin pad cards. Card with completed pp shows: yellow 'Puedes editar hasta: HH:MM' edit-window banner with testID pp-edit-{id} ✅; trash icon button testID pp-delete-{id} ✅; main button text 'Reemplazar foto' ✅. Tapping the trash button triggers window.confirm → DELETE /foto API → banner disappears, progress bar drops 1/2→0/2, main button reverts to 'Tomar foto', success toast 'Foto eliminada' ✅. ADMIN (1440x900): after finalizing the orden with lat=40.4168, lng=-3.7038, address='Plaza Mayor, Madrid', the admin orden detail renders the LocationMap inside 'Evidencia del trabajo' section: testID orden-close-map present ✅, iframe src is openstreetmap.org/export/embed.html with marker=40.4168 ✅. Note: button innerText query returned [] in my evaluate() probably because TouchableOpacity renders as <div role='button'> with deeply nested <span> wrappers — buttons are confirmed present via testIDs open-map-external and open-map-google in the source. Geolocation denial UI path NOT exercised end-to-end because the confirm flow requires uploading a real photo via the camera/file picker which is heavy to script; the denial logic was verified by reading the code (catch GeoError → showToast + abort, no API call). REACT NATIVE warnings (pre-existing): 'shadow*' style props deprecated — non-blocking.
action_items: OPTIONAL: The geolocation-denied path in confirmarPp shows a toast and aborts — works correctly per code review, but couldn't be exercised end-to-end via Playwright because the confirm action needs an uploaded photo first (camera/gallery picker). Consider adding a test-only injection seam to bypass the photo upload, OR an explicit data-testid wrapping the toast message so QA can verify the error toast text 'Has denegado el permiso…'.

LOW: LocationMap buttons (testID open-map-external, open-map-google) are correctly rendered via testID but their inner Text label spans are not picked up by querySelector('button, [role="button"]').innerText in evaluate() — minor RN-web rendering quirk, no fix needed; tests should select by testID.

INFO: The pre-existing 6 mitiendapro-related test failures in test_mvg_iter2.py (since iter1) are still failing. They are independent of iter7 and should be addressed separately by either (a) mapping WhatsApp 500 → mode='fallback_link' in whatsapp_service or (b) updating the test assertions to accept mode in {'fallback_link','error'}.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1519 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Strong candidate to split: move all /tecnico/ordenes/{id}/pinpad/* handlers + helper _can_edit into routers/tecnico_ordenes.py, and the suministros helpers already live in a sub-router. Suggested split saves ~250 lines.

_can_edit() relies on uploaded_at and falls back to completed_at — good defensive default. But OrdenFinalizar has no uploaded_at concept, so the finalizar endpoint cannot benefit from the same edit window. Consider extending the 30-min window to PATCH /finalizar as well, OR document that finalizar-only ordenes (no pin_pads) cannot edit closing photo.
delete_pinpad_photo clears lat/lng/address but NOT accuracy_m — minor inconsistency; should reset to None too (line ~1454).

The Pydantic model OrdenFinalizar marks lat/lng as REQUIRED float (no default), which means existing UI code paths that call finalizar without geo (e.g. the legacy 'finalizar' button without pin pads) will now break with 422. Confirmed only iter7-aware flows call it; double-check there are no other callers.

LocationMap iframe is loaded via openstreetmap.org/export/embed.html — that endpoint is unauthenticated and CORS-friendly, but does have a small request budget. For high-traffic admin pages consider switching to Leaflet/MapLibre with raster tiles or Mapbox if rate-limit becomes an issue.

Frontend api error-handling pattern from iter6 (Array.isArray(detail) crash) is still NOT centralized in /app/frontend/src/api/client.ts axios interceptor. If the new finalizar 422 fires from the técnico app, the same crash could reappear because frontend now reads response.data.detail directly. Recommend the one-line stringify fix in the axios interceptor that was suggested in iter6.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_mvg_backend.py (updated test_tecnico_only_sees_own_ordenes: send lat/lng on finalizar, added a 422-without-geo sub-assertion)

success_rate.backend: 100% iter7 (17/17). Overall pytest 94/100 (6 PRE-EXISTING mitiendapro-related failures unrelated to iter7)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on the exercised UI paths (técnico orden detail card, edit-window banner, delete button, button text change, toast, admin LocationMap iframe with correct coords)

seed_data_creation: Created via admin API: 1 técnico TEST_geo_d7039e@mvg.cl / Tecnico123! (id 6f21c3d1-…). Seeded via direct MongoDB write: 1 orden id 00f538cb-5c19-4f1d-ae62-b42f43d41d4a with 2 pin pads, marked with _test_marker='iter7_ui'. This orden is now FINALIZADA with closed_lat=40.4168, closed_lng=-3.7038, closed_address='Plaza Mayor, Madrid'. Pytest fixtures seed/clean their own ordenes per test (each test gets a fresh 2-pinpad orden via _seed_orden_with_pinpads). Foreign-técnico fixture creates+deletes TEST_foreign_{hex}@mvg.cl. NOTE: the iter7-UI técnico and orden are NOT auto-cleaned because they were used for live UI verification — main agent can DELETE /api/admin/tecnicos/6f21c3d1-a6bc-4c57-8b02-79094956719a + db.ordenes.deleteOne({_test_marker:'iter7_ui'}) when desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter7 backend tests: /app/backend/tests/test_geolocation_iter7.py — 17 tests, ~6s, all green. They directly insert ordenes into MongoDB to bypass the lack of pin_pads in the public POST /api/admin/ordenes endpoint, and use the session-scoped tecnico_user fixture from conftest.py as the owner. Each fresh_orden_2pp fixture is function-scoped so each test starts with a clean 2-pin-pad orden assigned to tecnico_user. Edit-window expiry is exercised by backdating uploaded_at in MongoDB to 31 min ago. For the UI: the técnico account TEST_geo_d7039e@mvg.cl / Tecnico123! is still present in the DB along with finalized orden 00f538cb-5c19-4f1d-ae62-b42f43d41d4a (testID 'orden-close-map' is mounted on the admin detail at /(admin)/ordenes/{id}). Reproduce admin map view by logging in as admin and visiting that path on desktop viewport.
rca_of_issue: No new bugs were found in iter7. Pre-existing iter6 bug (Array.isArray(detail) → React render crash on 422) was NOT yet fixed in the codebase as of this run — it remains a latent risk because OrdenFinalizar now returns 422 when lat/lng are missing, so any frontend caller that forgets to pass geo would surface the same crash. RECOMMEND main agent applies the one-line axios-interceptor stringify fix from iter6 RCA before next release.
